feat-5.name: Vanguard 1
feat-5.historicalSignificance: The oldest human-made object still orbiting Earth.
feat-5.description: Vanguard 1 was one of the earliest satellites launched during the Space Age and remains one of the longest-orbiting artificial objects ever created.

feat-11.name: Vanguard 2
feat-11.historicalSignificance: The world's first weather satellite candidate.
feat-11.description: Vanguard 2 was designed to measure global cloud cover. However, due to an axis wobble, its data was difficult to interpret.

feat-29.name: Tiros 1
feat-29.historicalSignificance: The first fully successful weather satellite in human history.
feat-29.description: Tiros 1 proved the feasibility of using satellites to observe weather patterns from space, taking over 22,000 cloud-cover pictures.

feat-634.name: Syncom 2 (A 26)
feat-634.historicalSignificance: The world's first active geosynchronous communication satellite.
feat-634.description: Syncom 2 demonstrated the feasibility of communications via geosynchronous satellites, transmitting television and phone calls.

feat-858.name: Syncom 3
feat-858.historicalSignificance: The world's first geostationary communication satellite.
feat-858.description: Syncom 3 was positioned over the Pacific Ocean and was famously used to telecast the 1964 Summer Olympics from Tokyo to the United States.

feat-1317.name: Intelsat 1-F1 (Early Bird)
feat-1317.historicalSignificance: The first commercial communications satellite placed in geostationary orbit.
feat-1317.description: Early Bird allowed the first live, simultaneous television, telephone, and telegraph transmissions between Europe and North America.

feat-2608.name: ATS 1
feat-2608.historicalSignificance: Pioneered meteorological imaging and emergency communications from GEO.
feat-2608.description: ATS 1 captured the first full-disk image of Earth showing weather patterns and served for years as a vital emergency communications link.

feat-3029.name: ATS 3
feat-3029.historicalSignificance: Captured the first color full-disk photograph of Earth.
feat-3029.description: ATS 3 conducted experiments in meteorology, space communications, and navigation, operating for over 34 years before retirement.

feat-4250.name: Skynet 1A
feat-4250.historicalSignificance: The United Kingdom's first military communication satellite.
feat-4250.description: Skynet 1A was launched to provide secure communications for the British Armed Forces. It remains in a drifting graveyard orbit.

feat-22675.name: Cosmos 2251
feat-22675.historicalSignificance: Famous for its historical collision with active Iridium 33 satellite.
feat-22675.description: Cosmos 2251 was a Russian communication satellite that collided with the active commercial Iridium 33 satellite in 2009, producing thousands of tracked fragments of space debris.

feat-27386.name: Envisat
feat-27386.historicalSignificance: The largest civilian Earth observation satellite ever built.
feat-27386.description: Envisat carried 10 scientific instruments to monitor Earth's atmosphere, oceans, land, and ice. Contact was unexpectedly lost in April 2012.

feat-4353.name: Nato 2A
feat-4353.historicalSignificance: First military communications satellite launched for NATO.
feat-4353.description: NATO 2A was designed to provide voice and data communications for NATO commanders. It is now parked in a graveyard orbit.
